# Roles and permissions

Huddle uses a combination of **roles** and **tags** (user flags) to control access.

[← Back to features](README.md)

## Roles

| Role | Description |
|------|-------------|
| **Admin** | Full access to the admin panel, user management, organisation settings, and all mentor capabilities |
| **Member** | Default role; can view and participate across the platform |

## Tags

Tags are flexible labels assigned to users by admins. Common tags include:

| Tag | Grants |
|-----|--------|
| **Mentor** | Access to the [Mentors](mentors.md) area, [form](forms.md) management, and [wiki](wiki.md) editing |
| **Committee** | View financial columns on [project reports](reports.md) |
| **Keyholder**, **Trustee**, etc. | Displayed on profiles; used for filtering and identification |

Admins manage tags from the [Admin](admin.md) panel.

## Project and event ownership

- **Project managers** — admins, or the member who created the project — can edit, delete, upload images, and manage volunteers.
- **Project leaders** — the assigned leader — can additionally manage the Finance tab on [projects](projects.md) (quotes, invoices, payment tracking).
- **Event organisers** — admins, or the member who created the event — can edit, delete, and manage the volunteer roster on [events](events.md).
